title: Kenyan Post-Workout Sweet Shaggy Nigiri
description: A unique sweet shaggy post-workout dish inspired by Kenyan cuisine, featuring extra virgin olive oil and nigiri-shaping. This recipe is friendly to low-sodium diets and is perfect for a quick recovery after a workout.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the rolled oats, dried pineapple, and unsweetened applesauce until well combined.
Add the honey, extra virgin olive oil, vanilla extract, shredded coconut, and salt-free seasoning to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Gradually add the water to the dough, stirring with a spoon until it reaches the desired consistency.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a small oval shape, reminiscent of nigiri sushi.
Place the nigiri on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to set.
Before serving, toast the nigiri in a toaster oven at 350°F for 5-7 minutes, or until lightly browned and crispy on the outside.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional shredded coconut if desired.
Serve with a side of fresh fruit or a drizzle of honey for added flavor.
To make nigiri rice, combine 1 cup of water with 1 cup of uncooked short-grain rice in a medium saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid, simmering for 15-20 minutes or until the water is absorbed and the rice is cooked.
Let the rice cool to room temperature before using it to shape the nigiri.
To make homemade salt-free seasoning, combine 1 cup of dried herbs and spices (such as thyme, rosemary, and black pepper) with 1/2 cup of salt-free seasoning mix in a small bowl and mix well.
